headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) After Recent Share Price Pullback
summary: If you are wondering whether Royal Caribbean Cruises is still good value after such a strong run in recent years, this article will walk through the numbers in a straightforward way. The stock last closed at US$278.08, with returns of 31.9% over the past year and 215.3% over five years, even though the last 7 days and 30 days showed declines of 10.6% and 15.4% and the year to date move sits at a 1.8% decline. Recent market attention has focused on Royal Caribbean Cruises as part of ongoing...

headline: Better Stock to Buy Right Now: Royal Caribbean vs. Viking Holdings
summary: Both companies have benefited from strong demand for cruise vacations.

headline: Royal Caribbean (RCL) Is Down 10.6% After Softer 2026 Outlook and Rising Geopolitical Risks
summary: In recent days, Royal Caribbean Cruises’ initial fiscal 2026 guidance was received as slightly disappointing, while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have raised concerns about fuel costs, itinerary disruptions, and overall travel demand across the cruise sector. At the same time, investors are weighing competitive pressure from a rival’s planned increase in Caribbean capacity and Royal Caribbean’s own product and itinerary changes, which could influence future pricing power and guest...
